key_departures.impact: Etienne was the unquestioned starter and the face of the backfield, rushing for 1,107 yards and 7 TDs in 2025 plus 36 catches for 292 yards. His departure creates a clear RB1 vacancy. Bhayshul Tuten is the presumed successor but has only 307 rushing yards on his resume.

Lloyd earned second-team All-Pro honors in 2025 with 81 tackles, 5 INTs, 1.5 sacks, and a 99-yard pick-six. He was the unquestioned defensive captain and signal-caller. His departure is the single largest need-creating event of Jacksonville's offseason — no internal replacement currently comes close to his production.

Newsome was mid-season trade acquisition from Cleveland who played 12 games and posted 29 tackles and 6 PDs. He was not a long-term fixture and his departure is partially offset by Brown's re-signing. CB depth takes a minor hit but CB2 is now solidified with Brown.
Wingard was a depth/special teams safety who played limited defensive snaps. Eric Murray remains under contract. Minor depth loss at S; does not create a new need.

Mundt was released by the Jaguars in a cap-saving move that freed $2.7M. He was a depth TE who had minimal offensive role. Quintin Morris was re-signed to fill blocking TE duties. No meaningful need created.

signings.analysis: Brown re-signed on a 3-year, $33M deal just before the legal tampering window opened. He led the team with 12 PBUs in 2025 and was one of the defense's most consistent players. With Travis Hunter locked in at CB1, re-signing Brown locks in a genuine CB2 starter at a below-market rate (he likely could have earned more in open market per ESPN). Three-year deal with real commitment. CB is not a draft need. Jacksonville now has arguably one of the better CB rooms in the AFC with Hunter, Brown, Jourdan Lewis, and Jarrian Jones.

Gardeck returned on a 2-year deal worth $6.5M (per OverTheCap/Big Cat Country). He plays a hybrid role — primarily special teams but did see edge rush usage in 2025, posting 2.5 sacks and 9 QB hits. His role may expand with Devin Lloyd gone. However, the contract is well below starter money and he is 31 years old. This is a depth/versatility retention, not a solution to EDGE depth concerns. EDGE remains a potential draft target.

Morris re-signed on a modest 1-year, $2M deal. He was valued primarily for his pass-blocking skills and became an integral offensive piece in that role. His re-signing keeps the blocking TE spot filled, but his contract structure signals a depth piece, not a featured TE. If the Jaguars have a pre-existing need at TE, this does not resolve it.

Rodriguez signed a 2-year, $10M deal (max $12M) with $6.2M fully guaranteed. He rushed for 500 yards and 6 TDs in 2025 for Washington and has a prior relationship with HC Liam Coen from Kentucky. He is expected to complement Bhayshul Tuten as the presumed RB1, not replace Etienne outright. The contract is mid-tier for depth RBs — $5M AAV is below the starter threshold. This partially addresses the RB void left by Etienne but does not fully resolve it. Tuten + Rodriguez is a credible but unproven starting duo; RB remains a potential draft target in the mid-rounds.

Dallas re-signed on a 1-year, $1.3M deal. He provided depth coverage when Bhayshul Tuten dealt with a finger injury late in 2025. Veteran minimum-level deal; depth signing only. Does not move the needle on the RB need picture.

Dickerson re-signed on a 1-year, $1.5M deal. He became a key rotational piece on the IDL after being promoted to the active roster mid-season in 2025. Near-minimum contract; quality depth but does not address any starter-level IDL need.

signings.previous_team: Las Vegas Raiders (via trade, re-signed via extension)
signings.analysis: Meyers was acquired at the 2025 trade deadline from Las Vegas for a 2026 4th and 6th round pick, then signed a 3-year, $60M extension with $40M guaranteed in December 2025. The $40M in guarantees and $20M AAV signals genuine starter-level investment. He immediately became the team's top receiver in 2025 (27 catches, 355 yards in 6 games with the Jaguars). This is a fully resolved WR position alongside Brian Thomas Jr. and Travis Hunter. Note: The 4th/6th round picks paid for this deal reduce 2026 draft capital.

Van Lanen signed a 3-year, $51M extension ($32.5M guaranteed, can reach $55.5M) in January 2026, locking up the starting LT through 2029. His versatility (starts at LT, LG, RG, RT) and the commitment level of this deal signals that OT/LT is solidified. $17M AAV is solidly in the starter tier for OT. HC Liam Coen specifically identified Van Lanen as the permanent LT going forward. OT is no longer a primary draft need.
Taylor re-signed on a 1-year, $1.2M deal. He appeared in 17 games in 2025, primarily as depth. Near-minimum contract; does not change the CB need picture in any meaningful way.
Braswell re-signed on an ERFA tender, 1-year, $1.1M. He had 14 tackles and 3 PDs in 2025. Exclusive rights tender; locks in developmental depth. No material impact on needs.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Devin Lloyd — 2025 second-team All-Pro (81 tackles, 5 INTs, 1.5 sacks) — departed for Carolina on a 3-year, $45M deal. No replacement was signed in free agency. Ventrell Miller and Jack Kiser are the internal options but neither is a proven starter. LB is now the team's most critical draft need — must be addressed in rounds 1-3 of the 2026 draft.

Travis Etienne signed a 4-year, $52M deal with New Orleans. The Jaguars replaced him with Chris Rodriguez Jr. (2yr/$10M, depth-tier AAV) and retained DeeJay Dallas ($1.3M). Bhayshul Tuten is the presumed RB1 but has only 307 career rushing yards. Rodriguez is a rotational back, not a proven feature back. RB escalates to a tier 2 mid-round draft need — likely rounds 3-5.

Montaric Brown re-signed on a 3-year, $33M deal, locking in the CB2 opposite Travis Hunter. Combined with Jourdan Lewis, Jarrian Jones, and Braswell, the Jaguars have outstanding CB depth. Greg Newsome's departure is immaterial given this depth. CB drops from a need to a luxury pick at best.

Jakobi Meyers signed a 3-year, $60M extension ($40M guaranteed) in December 2025. With Brian Thomas Jr. and Travis Hunter also under contract, Jacksonville has three legitimate starting-caliber receivers. WR is no longer a draft need.

Cole Van Lanen signed a 3-year, $51M extension ($32.5M guaranteed) cementing him as the starting LT. HC Coen publicly identified Van Lanen as the permanent LT. Walker Little remains on the roster as a swing tackle/potential trade asset. OT is no longer a primary draft need.

Matt Dickerson's re-signing (1yr/$1.5M) is strictly depth. With Arik Armstead possibly a cap casualty candidate and Austin Johnson departed, IDL depth/starter quality is still a concern. No change in tier — IDL remains a mid-round draft target.

Gardeck's re-signing (2yr/$6.5M) is rotational/depth. He is 31 years old and at below-starter money. Travon Walker remains as the primary EDGE. EDGE is still a legitimate mid-round draft target — no change in tier.

key_additions.headline: Slot receiver re-signed as starter; $40M guaranteed cost Jacksonville a 2026 4th and 6th round pick in trade
Versatile starting LT locked up through 2029, securing Trevor Lawrence's blindside
Team's top CB2 (12 PBUs in 2025) re-signed below market value, solidifying elite CB room opposite Travis Hunter
Rotational RB with Coen connection added to complement Bhayshul Tuten after Etienne's departure
Hybrid pass rusher/special teams ace retained; 2.5 sacks and 9 QB hits in 2025
Blocking TE retained on depth deal to anchor the run/pass-protection TE role

key_losses.headline: Second-team All-Pro LB (5 INTs, 99-yd pick-six) departs; biggest hole on the 2026 roster
Four-year Jaguar and 1,107-yard rusher in 2025 signs with hometown Saints; Tuten inherits RB1 role
Mid-season trade acquisition (12 games, 6 PDs) departs; impact offset by Brown re-signing and deep CB room
Depth/special teams safety departs on a 1-year deal; Eric Murray remains as starting S
Depth TE released for cap savings; Quintin Morris fills blocking TE role

notes: Travis Etienne contract: ESPN/Schefter reported 4yr/$52M; Big Cat Country/OverTheCap cites 4yr/$48M. Using ESPN/Schefter as primary source ($52M). Guarantees not yet reported publicly.
Devin Lloyd contract: Multiple sources confirm 3yr/$45M with Panthers. Guarantees not yet reported.
Montaric Brown contract: Brown's exact guarantees not publicly reported. AAV $11M falls in the starter tier for CB.

Dennis Gardeck contract: Big Cat Country/OverTheCap reports 2yr/$6.5M; Roundtable and Black and Teal report 2yr/$12M. Using the OverTheCap-sourced figure ($6.5M) as the primary source per quality hierarchy.

Cap space figures reflect a heavily restructured cap situation. Pre-FA space was approximately $100K-$110K after restructures of Lewis, Murray, and Mekari. OverTheCap shows the team slightly over cap as of March 2026 at -$1.1M; more moves expected.
The Jaguars sacrificed 2026 4th and 6th round picks in the Meyers trade. This reduces their draft capital, currently holding 11 picks in the 2026 draft per Big Cat Country.
